channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: UN expands arms embargo on Haiti to all types of arms and ammunition
item.description: UNITED NATIONS (AP) — The U.N. Security Council voted unanimously Friday to expand the arms embargo in Haiti to all types of weapons and ammunition, expressing grave concern at the extremely high levels of gang violence and criminal activities in the impoverished Caribbean nation.
item.dc:creator: EDITH M. LEDERER, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Texas was about to execute Robert Roberson. Then a last-ditch tactic bought him more time
item.description: HOUSTON (AP) — Robert Roberson, set to be the first person in the U.S. to be put to death for a murder conviction tied to a diagnosis of shaken baby syndrome, was nearly out of options to stop his execution in Texas. A state parole board, multiple lower courts and the U.S.
item.dc:creator: JUAN A. LOZANO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Judge unseals heavily redacted trove of evidence in Trump's 2020 election interference case
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The judge overseeing Donald Trump's 2020 election interference case made public Friday a heavily redacted trove of documents that provide a small glimpse into the evidence prosecutors will present if the case ever goes to trial.
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER and ALANNA DURKIN RICHER, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: 6 Florida counties saw 'unusual increase' in flesh-eating bacteria after hurricanes: health officials
item.description: Florida health officials noted that six Tampa Bay area counties saw an "unusual increase" in cases of Vibrio vulnificus this year, attributed to floodwaters from back-to-back hurricanes.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, Fox 5

item.title: GOP lawmakers seek investigation of consulting firm over its work in China
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A group of Republican lawmakers is demanding a federal investigation into the U.S. consulting firm McKinsey over its work with the Chinese government and state-owned enterprises, even as the company reportedly is revamping its China business to reduce risks.
item.dc:creator: DIDI TANG, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Video and an unused bullet prove man's guilt in Indiana girls' killings, prosecutor says
item.description: DELPHI, Ind. (AP) — A man charged with killing two teenage girls in a small Indiana community forced them off a hiking trail before cutting their throats, a prosecutor said Friday, telling jurors that the evidence includes images and audio recorded on a victim's phone.
item.dc:creator: RICK CALLAHAN, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Mexican cartel leader 'El Mayo' Zambada makes court appearance in his US drug trafficking case
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Ismael “El Mayo” Zambada, a longtime leader of Mexico’s Sinaloa cartel who is facing drug trafficking charges in a U.S. court, appeared for the first time Friday before the judge who is presiding over his case.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Bodycam shows wild Houston gunfight as officers respond to active home invasion robbery
item.description: Three men arrested after a Houston home invasion escalated into the ambush shooting of a responding officer as intruders held a family at gunpoint.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump tells child there will be no cows under Harris
item.description: Former President Donald Trump joined Fox and Friends on Friday for an in-person interview where he told a 6-year-old child that the U.S. “won’t have any cows” if Vice President Kamala Harris is elected.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, KREX 5

item.title: Leland Vittert’s War Notes: War on Musk
item.description: Just a few years ago, Democrats praised Elon Musk for saving humanity — electric cars, cheap space flight, the answer to rural broadband — but now he’s a James Bond supervillain who needs to be stopped at any cost.
item.dc:creator: Leland Vittert, NewsNation

item.title: Walter White of 'Breaking Bad' returns for New Mexico litter campaign
item.description: Two television ads featuring Bryan Cranston as Walter White, directed by "Breaking Bad" creator Vince Gilligan, were created to promote clean-up of New Mexico areas.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Salinas, Fox 5

item.title: Trump expresses frustration about calls to deploy Haley
item.description: “But let me just tell you: Nikki Haley and I fought, and I beat her by 50, 60, 90 points. I beat her in her own state. I beat Nikki badly.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump family will get 75% of crypto token revenue
item.description: The Trump family's cryptocurrency platform, World Liberty Financial, indicated in a report released Thursday that DT Marks DEFI LLC, a Delaware-based company connected to the former president, could get 75 percent of WLF's protocol revenues.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Abortion is on the ballot in 10 states. Here's where the measures stand
item.description: Voters in 10 states will decide this November whether to adopt constitutional amendments that could shape the future of abortion access in their states.
item.dc:creator: Alejandra O'Connell-Domenech, KRON4

item.title: Menendez brothers' uncle requests DA meeting; says boys' motive was greed
item.description: Anderson believes Erik and Lyle Menendez weren't molested by their father Jose and that "justice was served.
item.dc:creator: Liz Jassin, ABC 4

item.title: State troopers, NYPD descend on AOC's 'Red Light' neighborhood to clamp down on rampant prostitution, crime
item.description: New York state troopers were deployed to help the NYPD tackle rampant crime and prostitution in a Queens neighborhood, part of which is represented by Rep. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez, D-N.Y.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Missing teen kayaker found clinging to kayak off Waikiki
item.description: "I think he was in total shock because he wasn't emotional at all. I was actually crying my guts off because he was okay.
item.dc:creator: Nicole Napuunoa, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Almost 23% of people say politics influences where they live: Poll
item.description: "Some go so far as to consider moving to find that alignment," Danielle Hale, chief economist for Realtor.com, said.
item.dc:creator: Cassie Buchman, KRON4

item.title: 7-year-old crashes one school bus into another during apparent training in Missouri
item.description: She won't be able to drive for another nine years but parents of an Independence second grader say she's already had her first crash.
item.dc:creator: Dave D'Marko, ABC 4

item.title: Uphill battles that put abortion rights on ballots are unlikely to end even if the measures pass
item.description: Voters in nine states are deciding next month whether to add the right to abortion to their constitutions, but the measures are unlikely to dramatically change access — at least not immediately.
item.dc:creator: GEOFF MULVIHILL, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Liam Payne's family releases statement as more details, autopsy results emerge in singer's death
item.description: Payne, 31, died after a fall from the third story of the Casa Sur hotel in Buenos Aires on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, ABC 4

item.title: Mitzi Gaynor, ‘South Pacific’ star, dies at 93
item.description: Mitzi Gaynor, the effervescent dancer and actor who starred as Nellie Forbush in the 1958 film "South Pacific" and appeared in other musicals with Bing Crosby, Frank Sinatra and Gene Kelly, has died. She was 93.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Dollar General employee finds puppies dumped in trash amid cold Arkansas weather
item.description: Three puppies, less than a week old, were abandoned this week inside of a Dollar General Market dumpster, according to Arkansas authorities.
item.dc:creator: Elena Ramirez, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Instagram rolls out new features to prevent sextortion scams targeting teens
item.description: Instagram has introduced a series of new safety features to prevent sextortion schemes, marking the platform's latest effort to protect users, especially teenagers, from online harm.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, Fox 5

item.title: Amazon, Google make dueling nuclear investments to power data centers with clean energy
item.description: Amazon on Wednesday said that it was investing in small nuclear reactors, coming just two days after a similar announcement by Google, as both tech giants seek new sources of carbon-free electricity to meet surging demand from data centers and artificial intelligence.
item.dc:creator: ALEXA ST. JOHN and JENNIFER McDERMOTT, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: A Texas board rejects clemency plea from a man facing execution in shaken baby syndrome case
item.description: HOUSTON (AP) — A Texas state board on Wednesday declined to stop what could be the first execution in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: JUAN A. LOZANO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Pizza Hut unveils new restaurant — but there's a catch
item.description: Pizza Hut is unveiling a new restaurant — but there's a catch: There is only one location and it will only be open for two days. Also, you can only get one size of pizza. In honor of National Pizza Month, the chain is introducing the Personal Pan Pizza Hut.
item.dc:creator: Wil Day, KTLA 5

item.title: What to know about shaken baby syndrome as a Texas man could be first in US executed over it
item.description: HOUSTON (AP) — A Texas man this week could become the first person executed in the U.S. for a murder conviction tied to the diagnosis of shaken baby syndrome.
item.dc:creator: JUAN A. LOZANO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Minnesota school district bans officer from teaching after he reenacted George Floyd's murder
item.description: MINNEAPOLIS (AP) — A Minnesota school district has banned a police officer from working as a substitute teacher after a series of “racially harmful” actions that officials say included putting a student on the ground for a reenactment of the 2020 murder of George Floyd by a Minneapolis officer.
item.dc:creator: BY STEVE KARNOWSKI, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Killer's escape from Pennsylvania jail costs him his right to appeal life sentence
item.description: HARRISBURG, Pa. (AP) — A convicted killer’s escape from a Pennsylvania jail last year has cost him his right to appeal his original life sentence, a state court ruled Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Listeria recall grows to 12 million pounds of meat and poultry, some of it sent to US schools
item.description: A nationwide recall of meat and poultry products potentially contaminated with listeria has expanded to nearly 12 million pounds and now includes ready-to-eat meals sent to U.S. schools, restaurants and major retailers, federal officials said.
item.dc:creator: JONEL ALECCIA, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Clint Eastwood's daughter arrested in Beverly Hills in domestic violence case
item.description: Actor Francesca Eastwood, daughter of legendary actor Clint Eastwood, was arrested in Beverly Hills over the weekend on suspicion of felony domestic violence, according to reporting from the Los Angeles Times.
item.dc:creator: Josh DuBose, Fox 5

item.title: Navy finds wreckage of fighter jet that crashed during training in Washington state
item.description: The U.S. Navy located the wreckage Wednesday of a fighter jet that crashed in Washington state during a routine training flight in mountainous terrain. The two crew members onboard remain missing.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Protesters demand Kellogg remove artificial colors from Froot Loops and other cereals
item.description: Dozens of people have rallied outside the Michigan headquarters of WK Kellogg Co. to demand that the company remove artificial dyes from their cereals in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Suter, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Small business disaster loan program is out of money until Congress approves new funds
item.description: House Speaker Mike Johnson assured there would be support to provide necessary funds – when Congress returns after the November election.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Menendez brothers' family push for their release as prosecutors review 1989 case
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — The extended family of Erik and Lyle Menendez will advocate for the brothers' release from prison during a news conference set for Wednesday in downtown Los Angeles as prosecutors review new evidence to determine whether they should be serving life sentences for killing their pare
item.dc:creator: STEFANIE DAZIO, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Trump edges out Harris with early voters in battleground states
item.description: Former President Trump has a slight edge over Vice President Harris with early voters in battleground states, new polling shows, a promising sign for the Republican in the highly competitive presidential race.
item.dc:creator: Julia Mueller, WGN 9
